Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/onlyoffice-bin.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-7bc2tldz...
Cloned repository to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-7bc2tldz
Analyzing onlyoffice-bin AUR package (working in /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-7bc2tldz) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a standard binary package for OnlyOffice, sourcing a `.deb` file from the official GitHub releases page (`https://github.com/ONLYOFFICE/DesktopEditors/releases`). The `source` array uses a direct HTTPS link with a pinned version and architecture, and the checksums are explicitly defined and not set to 'SKIP', ensuring integrity verification. There are no command substitutions, variable expansions, or external executions in the global scope or within `pkgver()` — in fact, `pkgver` is statically defined, so no dynamic version fetching occurs.

The top-level content of the PKGBUILD consists only of safe variable assignments. No code is executed during sourcing beyond what is necessary for packaging metadata. The `prepare()` and `package()` functions are not executed under `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`, so they are out of scope for this narrow gate. The patch file is locally referenced and included in checksums, posing no risk during source download or PKGBUILD parsing.

No obfuscated code, network exfiltration, or remote code execution vectors are present in the global scope or in `pkgver()`. Fetching the sources involves only downloading a verified `.deb` and a patch file from trusted locations as defined by the package.
</details>
<summary>
No dangerous code in global scope or pkgver
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No dangerous code in global scope or pkgver[/]
Running makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare to download sources for agent...
makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare successful
Running makepkg --printsrcinfo to get source files for agent...
makepkg --printsrcinfo successful
Reviewing required files...
[0/2] Reviewing PKGBUILD...
[0/2] Reviewing PKGBUILD, 010-onlyoffice-bin-fix-document-opening.patch...
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD fetches a pre-built .deb package from the official GitHub releases page of ONLYOFFICE Desktop Editors, which is a legitimate and expected source for this binary package. The file is verified using a hardcoded SHA-256 checksum (`4271434e81be42b1559fd989d24bf6d413482f278dc6a5e328202a4fc1a18649`), ensuring integrity. The package extraction and patching process is standard for AUR binary packages: it uses `bsdtar` to extract the Debian package contents and applies a local patch to fix document opening behavior, both performed in a sandboxed build directory.

No suspicious network operations, code execution, or obfuscated commands are present. The `prepare()` and `package()` functions perform routine operations—extracting and copying files—with no evidence of data exfiltration, remote code download, or tampering with system files outside the package directory. The `options` array disables debug info, stripping, and empty directories, which is common for binary packages to preserve original structure and reduce size.

All dependencies and optdepends are standard desktop or multimedia libraries, consistent with an office suite that supports document rendering, accessibility, and embedded media. The package provides `onlyoffice=${pkgver}` and conflicts with the non-binary `onlyoffice` package, preventing conflicts. No maintainer scripts or hooks perform unexpected actions.
</details>
<summary>
Standard binary packaging from official source with checksum verification
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[1/2] Reviewing 010-onlyoffice-bin-fix-document-opening.patch...
+ Reviewed PKGBUILD. Status: SAFE -- Standard binary packaging from official source with checksum verification
LLM auditresponse for 010-onlyoffice-bin-fix-document-opening.patch:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The provided patch modifies the `Exec` line in the OnlyOffice desktop entry to change `%U` (URLs) to `%F` (files). This is a legitimate and common packaging adjustment to ensure file paths are passed correctly when opening documents, improving compatibility and user experience. The change aligns with desktop entry specifications and does not introduce any malicious behavior.

There are no suspicious network operations, unexpected file manipulations, or obfuscated commands. The patch only alters a desktop file for proper application invocation, which is a standard practice in AUR packages. No evidence of supply chain tampering or injected malicious code is present.
</details>
<summary>
Harmless desktop file execution parameter fix</summary>
</security_assessment>

[2/2] Reviewing ...
+ Reviewed 010-onlyoffice-bin-fix-document-opening.patch. Status: SAFE -- Harmless desktop file execution parameter fix
Reviewed all required files.
Deciding which files to review next...
Decided to review 1 additional files: .nvchecker.toml
Reviewing additional files...
Reviewing additional files...
[0/1] Reviewing .nvchecker.toml...
LLM auditresponse for .nvchecker.toml:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The `.nvchecker.toml` file is a configuration for nvchecker, a version checking tool commonly used in AUR packaging to monitor upstream releases. The configuration defines a single source for the `onlyoffice-bin` package: a Git repository at `https://github.com/ONLYOFFICE/DesktopEditors.git`, with version tags prefixed by "v". This is a standard and expected practice in AUR packages, as it allows maintainers to automatically track new upstream releases by observing Git tags.

There are no signs of malicious behavior in this file. It does not execute code, download binaries, or perform network operations beyond what is typical for version tracking. The use of a public, documented upstream Git repository—consistent with ONLYOFFICE's official source—further supports its legitimacy. No obfuscation, suspicious URLs, or data exfiltration mechanisms are present.

This file serves a purely declarative purpose and poses no security risk on its own.
</details>
<summary>
Standard version-checking config, no malicious content
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[1/1] Reviewing ...
+ Reviewed .nvchecker.toml. Status: SAFE -- Standard version-checking config, no malicious content
Reviewed all additional files.
Reviewed additional files.
Audit complete! Result: No issues found

There is no obfuscation, no external downloads, no system modification beyond the intended packaging fix, and no malicious intent.
</details>
